title: geranium pratense subsp. transbaicalicum
intro: Geranium pratense subsp. transbaicalicum, also known as Trans-Baikal cranesbill, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Geraniaceae. It is native to the mountainous regions of eastern Asia and has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Geranium pratense subsp. transbaicalicum include reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and promoting wound healing, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various health concerns. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and its extracts can be used in ointments and salves for topical applications.
